danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist; represents legal/authoritarian threat to Toontown
Existential threat to cartoon characters; dissolution/death mechanic
Enemy encounters; enforcers of Judge Doom's will

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones; refuge from real-world threats and antagonists
Playable character; protagonist ally; signal of narrative safety/progression

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Toontown district; whimsical, animated aesthetic contrasting live-action world

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Weasel henchmen as visual representation of Doom's authority
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Judicial/authoritarian spaces; Dip processing facilities; real-world industrial zones
Realistic urban environments; LA locations; industrial architecture

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cartoon characters behave cautiously; increased enemy encounters near Toontown boundaries
Roger Rabbit investigates crime scenes; dialogue reveals plot regarding Judge Doom's conspiracy against Toons
Gameplay mechanics punish exposure to Dip; represents existential threat to cartoon existence

environmental_storytelling.example: Movement between Toontown and human LA areas communicates cultural/physical separation between cartoon and live-action worlds
Judge Doom's authority manifests through judicial spaces, Weasel patrols, and Dip manufacturing sites
Visual contrast between whimsical cartoon environments and noir-influenced live-action city locations establishes tension and world-state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Orchestrates conspiracy; commands Weasel forces; pursues elimination of Toons via Dip
Patrol environments; attack player; block progression; represent Doom's reach
Investigates, solves puzzles, avoids Dip exposure, interacts with allies
Seek safety in Toontown; provide assistance; represent what player protects

notes: Available source is a disambiguation page with minimal game-specific details. The 1989 NES version by Rare/LJN Toys is confirmed but substantive game mechanics, visual design, and world-communication methods are not documented in provided source. Extraction based on source material IP context (film narrative) and logical extrapolation of adventure game paradigm rather than direct empirical game data.
